summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in enterprise AI adoption, AI-powered customer service, and AI-driven innovations. Key players like Anthropic, OpenAI, and NVIDIA are making strides in AI implementation, research, and hardware development.
insight: The increasing focus on AI implementation and enterprise adoption suggests a shift towards practical applications of AI, moving beyond just model development.
highlights: Anthropic-backed Ode launches to accelerate enterprise AI adoption
OpenAI's potential smart speaker device could revolutionize voice-based interactions
NVIDIA and Japan collaborate to bring full-stack AI and robotics to various industries

articles.title: Anthropic, Blackstone bet the next trillion-dollar AI business is implementation, not models
articles.description: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Indian AI coding startup Emergent becomes a unicorn with $130M Series C
articles.description: The startup has reached a $120 million annualized revenue run rate and more than 200,000 paying customers.

articles.title: NVIDIA and Japan Bring Full-Stack AI and Robotics to Every Industry
articles.description: Home to leading manufacturers, robotics pioneers and infrastructure builders, Japan is one of the world’s centers of AI — building across the full stack with NVIDIA technologies. N

articles.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
articles.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

articles.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
articles.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like

articles.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
articles.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

articles.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
articles.description: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

articles.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
articles.description: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

articles.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
articles.description: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engine

articles.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
articles.description: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of hig

articles.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
articles.description: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

articles.title: Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US
articles.description:
Demis Hassabis, during a panel session at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland. | Image: Bloomberg via Getty Images	

Demis Hassabis thinks the world needs an AI watchdog
